ocChatErrorRateLimit: Rate limit reached (429 Too Many Requests). Please wait a moment and try again.
ocChatErrorOutputLength: Response reached the maximum output length.
ocChatStalled: Waiting for a response… The provider may be rate-limiting requests.
ocChatErrorTimeout: No response (timed out). Please wait a moment and try again.
rulesDescription: Configure custom instructions for OpenCode. By creating an AGENTS.md file, you can provide custom instructions to OpenCode. These instructions are included in the LLM context to customize LLM behavior for specific projects.
toolsDescription: Manage the tools available to the LLM. Tools allow the LLM to perform actions in your codebase. OpenCode comes with a set of built-in tools, but you can extend it with custom tools or MCP servers.
toolDesc_bash: Execute shell commands in the project environment
toolDesc_edit: Edit files precisely by replacing exact matching text
toolDesc_read: Read files and return their contents
toolDesc_write: Create new files or overwrite existing files
toolDesc_grep: Search file contents with full regex syntax and file pattern filtering
toolDesc_list: List files and directories in a directory
toolDesc_todowrite: Manage a todo list during coding
toolDesc_websearch: Web search powered by Exa AI
toolDesc_question: Ask a question to the user during execution
agentsDescription: Agents are specialized AI assistants that can be configured for specific tasks and workflows. They allow you to create focused tools with custom prompts, models, and tool access.
commandsDescription: Create custom commands for repetitive tasks. Custom commands let you specify a prompt to run when that command is executed in the TUI.
commandNameHint: Use the command by typing '/' followed by the command name.
commandDescriptionHint: Enter a brief description of what the command does.
commandTemplateHint: Define the prompt sent to the LLM when the command is executed.
commandAgentHint: Specify the agent to run this command.
commandModelHint: Override the default model for this command.
mcpDescription: Add local and remote MCP tools. You can add external tools to OpenCode using the Model Context Protocol (MCP). OpenCode supports both local and remote servers.
mcpScopeCopyOrMoveMessage: "{{name}}" is currently global. Copy to project (keep global), or move (remove from global)?
skillsDescription: Agent skills let OpenCode discover reusable instructions from your repo or home directory. Skills are loaded on-demand via the native skill tool and saved as SKILL.md files.
skillNamePatternError: Lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only (e.g. my-skill)
skillDescriptionHint: Enter a brief description of what the skill does.
customToolsDescription: Custom tools are functions you create that the LLM can call during conversations. They work alongside OpenCode's built-in tools like read, write, and bash.
agentAddRequiredToolConfirm: The "{{agent}}" agent needs the {{tools}} custom tool to work. Add it now?
pluginsDescription: Manage opencode plugins. Plugins are loaded at server startup and can add agents, tools, and skills.
pluginSpec: Package spec (npm name / git URL / local path)
pluginRestartNotice: Restarting the opencode server is required to apply plugin changes. Restarting stops the server; it relaunches with the new plugin configuration on your next chat.
pluginDesc_superpowers: Superpowers — extends opencode with a library of skills (TDD, debugging, brainstorming, and more).
pluginDesc_oh-my-opencode: Oh My OpenCode — a batteries-included plugin bundling curated agents, tools, and MCPs.
